name: Blanton's Single Barrel
mash: Corn, rye, malted barley (BT mash bill)
notes: maple, honey, vanilla, orange citrus, stewed berries, caramel, toffee, baking spice
best: Relaxed pace, medium cigar, member new to bourbon or wants approachable single barrel.
avoid: User wants long-aged wheated collector profile or maximum intensity.
why: Recommend as the Novice journey first stop — citrus, spice, and toffee without allocated weight.
catalogLine: Blanton's Single Barrel · Kentucky Straight Bourbon · $31 · Whiskey Journey: Novice · Notes: orange zest, baking spice, toffee, caramel
blurb: Blanton's Single Barrel — maple, honey, and vanilla with orange citrus, stewed berries, caramel, and toffee. Official 93-proof BT mash bill #1 entry.

name: Pappy Van Winkle 23yr
notes: caramel, ripe apple, cherry, oak, tobacco, chocolate
best: Collector pour, cocoa-leaning cigar, member names Pappy or wants Advanced journey.
avoid: Beginner palate, budget-only, or user needs short/low-intensity pour.
why: Recommend when the member wants allocated prestige, orchard fruit over oak, or a long sweet caramel finish beside a maduro.
catalogLine: Pappy Van Winkle 23yr · Kentucky Straight Bourbon · $85 · 95.6 proof (47.8% ABV) · wheated mash · Whiskey Journey: Advanced · Notes: caramel, ripe apples, cherries, oak, tobacco, hint of chocolate
blurb: Pappy Van Winkle 23yr — caramel, ripe apple, and cherry over oak, tobacco, and chocolate into a long sweet caramel finish. Distillery-official wheated profile.
awards: SF World Spirits Gold 2019; IWSC Silver 2019 (Star Attraction); USC Excellent 90–94 pts 2018; NYWWSC Best Bourbon 2018; Pacult 5 Stars 2018; World Whiskies Awards Overall Winner 2018

name: 1792 Small Batch Bourbon
avoid: User wants wheated or very mild.
why: Recommend when cinnamon and pepper should echo a spicy cigar — high-proof without Booker's price.
catalogLine: 1792 Small Batch Bourbon · Kentucky · $32 · 93.7 proof (46.9% ABV) · Journey: Intermediate · Notes: caramel, vanilla
blurb: 1792 Small Batch — cinnamon and pepper over caramel at nearly 94 proof. Value bourbon that still feels serious beside a Corojo.

name: Four Roses Single Barrel
mash: High-rye bourbon (OBSV/OESK recipes vary by barrel)
notes: dried spice, pear, cocoa, vanilla, maple syrup, ripe plum, cherries
best: Bourbon nerd, single-barrel interest, spice-forward cigar.
why: Recommend for high-rye fruit and spice beside a medium-full cigar — cherry and baking spice without peat.
catalogLine: Four Roses Single Barrel · Kentucky · $45 · 100 proof (50.0% ABV) · Journey: Intermediate · Notes: cherry, spice
blurb: Four Roses Single Barrel — cherry and spice from a high-rye recipe. The bourbon pour that earns respect from enthusiasts without collector hype.

name: Aberlour 12 Year
notes: golden honey, pear, vanilla toffee, orange, red apple, liquorice, almond, spice
best: Sherry lover, cherry-chocolate cigar, Speyside value.
why: Recommend when sherry sweetness should meet a maduro — black cherry, chocolate, toffee.
catalogLine: Aberlour 12 Year · Speyside, Scotland · $55 · 86 proof (43.0% ABV) · Notes: sherry, black cherry
blurb: Aberlour 12 — sherry, black cherry, and chocolate in a Speyside pour. Sherry depth without only reaching for Macallan.

name: Angel's Envy Kentucky Straight Bourbon
mash: Corn, rye, malt; port pipe finish
notes: vanilla, raisins, maple syrup, toasted nuts, ripe fruit, toast, bitter chocolate, madeira
best: Member wants finished bourbon, cocoa-leaning cigar, conversation pour.
avoid: User wants dry or unpeated only.
why: Recommend when port sweetness and cocoa in the glass should echo a maduro — raisin, vanilla, and oak.
catalogLine: Angel's Envy Kentucky Straight Bourbon · Kentucky · $55 · 86.6 proof (43.3% ABV) · Journey: Intermediate · Notes: port wine, cocoa
blurb: Angel's Envy — port-finished bourbon with cocoa and raisin over caramel. The finished bourbon cigar lounges keep coming back to.

name: Appleton Estate 21 Year
notes: floral, vanilla, orange peel, nutmeg, coffee, cocoa, dry finish
best: Rum collector, funky Jamaican ask, celebration cigar.
why: Recommend when the member wants Jamaican character — orange, oak, and noble funk.
catalogLine: Appleton Estate 21 Year · Jamaica · $120 · 86 proof (43.0% ABV) · Notes: orange, vanilla
blurb: Appleton Estate 21 — orange and vanilla over Jamaican funk and oak. Luxury rum with personality beyond the sweet shelf.

name: Ardbeg 10 Year
notes: peat smoke, lemon, lime, dark chocolate, menthol, black pepper, tar, espresso, aniseed, almond
best: Smoke maximalist, Lagavulin wasn't enough, bold maduro.
why: Recommend when the member wants the peatiest pour on the rail — lime, espresso, and charcoal smoke.
catalogLine: Ardbeg 10 Year · Islay, Scotland · $55 · 92 proof (46.0% ABV) · Notes: peat smoke, lime
blurb: Ardbeg 10 — lime and espresso riding a wave of peat. For the member who told you Lagavulin was too subtle.

name: Balvenie 14 Year Caribbean Cask
notes: toffee, fresh fruit, vanilla, sweet oak, ginger, candied fruit
best: Finished Scotch curious, rum-cask interest, celebratory pour.
avoid: User wants dry or smoky only.
why: Recommend when rum-cask sweetness should complement a medium-full cigar — toffee, vanilla, apple.
catalogLine: Balvenie 14 Year Caribbean Cask · Speyside, Scotland · $75 · 86 proof (43.0% ABV) · Notes: toffee, vanilla
blurb: Balvenie 14 Caribbean Cask — toffee and rum sweetness from a Speyside finish. Scotch that borrows rum's friendship with cigars.

name: Basil Hayden's Kentucky Straight Bourbon
best: Mild cigar, first bourbon, user intimidated by proof.
avoid: User wants cask-strength or heavy flavor.
why: Recommend when the member wants a gentle bourbon beside a mild cigar — peppermint, honey, and light spice.
catalogLine: Basil Hayden's Kentucky Straight Bourbon · Kentucky · $45 · 80 proof (40.0% ABV) · Journey: Novice · Notes: peppermint, tea
blurb: Basil Hayden's — light, peppery, and approachable. The bourbon for a Connecticut wrapper when 100+ proof feels like too much.

name: Booker's Bourbon
research: Batch proof varies by batch; 125 proof typical. Official Booker's profile vanilla and spice.
best: Experienced bourbon drinker, bold cigar, wants proof.
why: Recommend when the member wants cask-strength bourbon for a maduro — caramel, oak, and serious heat.
catalogLine: Booker's Bourbon · Kentucky · $85 · 125 proof (62.5% ABV) · Journey: Advanced · Notes: caramel, vanilla
blurb: Booker's — uncut Kentucky strength with caramel and peanut brittle over oak. Built for a full-bodied cigar when you want the glass to match the stick.

name: Buffalo Trace Kentucky Straight Bourbon
mash: Corn, rye, malted barley (BT mash bill #1)
notes: vanilla, mint, molasses, brown sugar, spice, oak, toffee, dark fruit, anise
best: Bourbon beginner, first pour, user wants approachable sweetness without heat.
avoid: User wants peat, high-proof spice bomb, or allocated wheated collector only.
why: Recommend when the member is new to bourbon or asks what to start with — vanilla, caramel, gentle oak.
catalogLine: Buffalo Trace Kentucky Straight Bourbon · Kentucky · $28 · 90 proof (45% ABV) · Journey: Novice · Notes: vanilla, caramel
blurb: Buffalo Trace — vanilla, mint, and molasses over brown sugar, toffee, and dark fruit. The distillery's official teaching pour for bourbon newcomers.

name: Bulleit Kentucky Straight Bourbon
notes: spice, sweet oak, maple, nutmeg, toffee
best: High-rye curious, classic cocktail, recognizable shelf pour.
why: Recommend as approachable high-rye bourbon — dried fruit and spice without Booker's heat.
catalogLine: Bulleit Kentucky Straight Bourbon · Kentucky · $32 · 90 proof (45.0% ABV) · Journey: Novice · Notes: dried fruit, vanilla
blurb: Bulleit Bourbon — high-rye spice and dried fruit in a pour every bar stocks. Easy to recommend when someone just says bourbon.

name: Casa Dragones Joven
notes: floral, citrus, sweet roasted agave, vanilla, spice, pear, hazelnut
best: Ultra-luxury tequila, celebration, member names Casa Dragones.
why: Recommend when the member wants top-shelf tequila beyond 1942 — crisp, floral, luxury agave.
catalogLine: Casa Dragones Joven · Jalisco, Mexico · $285 · 80 proof (40.0% ABV) · Notes: citrus, pepper
blurb: Casa Dragones Joven — crisp luxury agave with floral lift. The tequila pour for members who already own Clase Azul and want what insiders drink.
